name: :book: A curated list of resources dedicated to Natural Language Processing (NLP)
description: :book: A curated list of resources dedicated to Natural Language Processing (NLP)

links.title: ACL Anthology
links.description: canonical archive of papers from ACL, EMNLP, NAACL, EACL, COLING, and related venues.

links.title: NLP-Progress
links.description: tracks state-of-the-art results across common NLP tasks and datasets.

links.title: Papers With Code: NLP
links.description: papers, benchmarks, and leaderboards for NLP tasks.

links.title: Sebastian Ruder's newsletter
links.description: regular roundups of NLP research and trends.

links.title: ACL Rolling Review
links.description: the rolling review process feeding ACL-affiliated venues.

links.title: The Gradient
links.description: long-form essays on ML and NLP research.

links.title: Visual NLP Paper Summaries
links.description: illustrated summaries of recent papers.

links.title: NLP's ImageNet moment has arrived
links.description: 2018 essay on the rise of pretrained language models.

links.title: Survey of the State of the Art in Natural Language Generation
links.description: 2017 NLG survey.

links.title: The Berkeley NLP Group
links.description: Notable contributions include a tool to reconstruct long dead languages, referenced [here](https://www.bbc.com/news/science-environment-21427896) and by taking corpora from 637 languages currently spoken in Asia and the Pacific and recreating their descendant.

links.title: Language Technologies Institute, Carnegie Mellon University
links.description: Notable projects include [Avenue Project](http://www.cs.cmu.edu/~avenue/), a syntax driven machine translation system for endangered languages like Quechua and Aymara and previously, [Noah's Ark](http://www.cs.cmu.edu/~ark/) which created [AQMAR](http://www.cs.cmu.edu/~ark/AQMAR/) to improve NLP tools for Arabic.

links.title: NLP research group, Columbia University
links.description: Responsible for creating BOLT ( interactive error handling for speech translation systems) and an un-named project to characterize laughter in dialogue.

links.title: The Center or Language and Speech Processing, John Hopkins University
links.description: Recently in the news for developing speech recognition software to create a diagnostic test or Parkinson's Disease, [here](https://www.clsp.jhu.edu/2019/03/27/speech-recognition-software-and-machine-learning-tools-are-being-used-to-create-diagnostic-test-for-parkinsons-disease/#.XNFqrIkzYdU).

links.title: Computational Linguistics and Information Processing Group, University of Maryland
links.description: Notable contributions include [Human-Computer Cooperation or Word-by-Word Question Answering](http://www.umiacs.umd.edu/~jbg/projects/IIS-1652666) and modeling development of phonetic representations.

links.title: Penn Natural Language Processing, University of Pennsylvania
links.description: famous for creating the [Penn Treebank](https://catalog.ldc.upenn.edu/LDC99T42) and the [Penn Discourse Treebank](https://www.cis.upenn.edu/~pdtb/).

links.title: AI Playbook
links.description: a16z AI playbook is a great link to forward to your managers or content for your presentations

links.title: NLP in Python
links.description: Collection of Github notebooks

links.title: Natural Language Processing: An Introduction
links.description: Oxford

links.title: Hands-On NLTK Tutorial
links.description: NLTK Tutorials, Jupyter notebooks

links.title: Natural Language Processing with Python – Analyzing Text with the Natural Language Toolkit
links.description: An online and print book introducing NLP concepts using NLTK. The book's authors also wrote the NLTK library.

links.title: Train a new language model from scratch
links.description: Hugging Face 🤗

links.title: Advanced NLP with spaCy
links.description: Free online course covering text processing, large-scale data analysis, processing pipelines, and training neural network models for custom NLP tasks.

links.title: Kaggle NLP Learning Guide
links.description: Beginner-friendly tutorials including getting started guides, deep learning for NLP, and visual explanations of techniques like BERT, GloVe, and TF-IDF.

links.title: The Illustrated BERT, ELMo, and co. (How NLP Cracked Transfer Learning)
arXiv: Natural Language Processing (Almost) from Scratch
Machine Learning Mastery: Deep Learning for Natural Language Processing
Advanced Natural Language Processing
links.description: CS 685, UMass Amherst CS

links.title: Deep Natural Language Processing
links.description: Lectures series from Oxford

links.title: Deep Learning for Natural Language Processing (cs224-n)
links.description: Richard Socher and Christopher Manning's Stanford Course

links.title: Neural Networks for NLP
links.description: Carnegie Mellon Language Technology Institute there

links.title: fast.ai Code-First Intro to Natural Language Processing
links.description: This covers a blend of traditional NLP topics (including regex, SVD, naive bayes, tokenization) and recent neural network approaches (including RNNs, seq2seq, GRUs, and the Transformer), as well as addressing urgent ethical issues, such as bias and disinformation. Find the Jupyter Notebooks [here](https://github.com/fastai/course-nlp)

links.title: DeepLearning.AI Natural Language Processing Specialization
links.description: 4-course program covering sentiment analysis, word embeddings, RNNs, LSTMs, attention mechanisms, and Transformer models like BERT and T5 for tasks including machine translation and summarization.

links.title: Stanford CS336: Language Modeling from Scratch
links.description: end-to-end course on building language models, including data, tokenization, training, and evaluation.

links.title: Stanford CS25: Transformers United
links.description: seminar series with guest lectures from authors of recent transformer and NLP research.

links.title: Cohere LLM University
links.description: free course on LLMs, embeddings, semantic search, and NLP applications.

links.title: Hugging Face NLP Course
links.description: hands-on NLP with Transformers, Datasets, and Tokenizers libraries.

links.title: NLP Demystified
links.description: Free beginner-friendly course covering NLP fundamentals through transformers, with Python/Jupyter notebooks.

links.title: Speech and Language Processing
links.description: free, by Prof. Dan Jurafsy

links.title: Natural Language Processing
links.description: free, NLP notes by Dr. Jacob Eisenstein at GeorgiaTech

links.title: NLP with PyTorch
links.description: Brian & Delip Rao

links.title: Natural Language Processing with Spark NLP
Real-World Natural Language Processing
links.description: by Masato Hagiwara

links.title: Natural Language Processing in Action, Second Edition
links.description: by Hobson Lane and Maria Dyshel

links.title: Transformers in Action
links.description: by Nicole Koenigstein

links.title: Knwl.js
links.description: A Natural Language Processor in JS

links.title: Poplar
links.description: A web-based annotation tool for natural language processing (NLP)

links.title: node-question-answering
links.description: Fast and production-ready question answering w/ DistilBERT in Node.js

links.title: TextAttack
links.description: Adversarial attacks, adversarial training, and data augmentation in NLP

links.title: TextBlob
links.description: Providing a consistent API for diving into common natural language processing (NLP) tasks. Stands on the giant shoulders of [Natural Language Toolkit (NLTK)](https://www.nltk.org/) and [Pattern](https://github.com/clips/pattern), and plays nicely with both :+1:

links.title: gensim
links.description: Python library to conduct unsupervised semantic modelling from plain text :+1:

links.title: scattertext
links.description: Python library to produce d3 visualizations of how language differs between corpora

links.title: PyTorch-NLP
links.description: NLP research toolkit designed to support rapid prototyping with better data loaders, word vector loaders, neural network layer representations, common NLP metrics such as BLEU

links.title: foliapy
links.description: Python library for working with [FoLiA](https://proycon.github.io/folia/), an XML format for linguistic annotation.

links.title: jPTDP
links.description: A toolkit for joint part-of-speech (POS) tagging and dependency parsing. jPTDP provides pre-trained models for 40+ languages.

links.title: Chazutsu
links.description: A library for downloading&parsing standard NLP research datasets

links.title: Word Forms
links.description: Word forms can accurately generate all possible forms of an English word

links.title: Multilingual Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA)
links.description: A multilingual and extensible document clustering pipeline

links.title: NLP Architect
links.description: A library for exploring the state-of-the-art deep learning topologies and techniques for NLP and NLU

links.title: Flair
links.description: A very simple framework for state-of-the-art multilingual NLP built on PyTorch. Includes BERT, ELMo and Flair embeddings.

links.title: Kashgari
links.description: Simple, Keras-powered multilingual NLP framework, allows you to build your models in 5 minutes for named entity recognition (NER), part-of-speech tagging (PoS) and text classification tasks. Includes BERT and word2vec embedding.

links.title: FARM
links.description: Fast & easy transfer learning for NLP. Harvesting language models for the industry. Focus on Question Answering.

links.title: Rita DSL
links.description: a DSL, loosely based on [RUTA on Apache UIMA](https://uima.apache.org/ruta.html). Allows to define language patterns (rule-based NLP) which are then translated into [spaCy](https://spacy.io/), or if you prefer less features and lightweight - regex patterns.

links.title: corex_topic
links.description: Hierarchical Topic Modeling with Minimal Domain Knowledge

links.title: Sockeye
links.description: Neural Machine Translation (NMT) toolkit that powers Amazon Translate.

links.title: Jury
links.description: Evaluation of NLP model outputs offering various automated metrics.

links.title: Pearmut
links.description: Human annotation tool for multilingual NLP tasks, such as machine translation.

links.title: Sentence-Transformers
links.description: sentence/document embeddings, semantic search, and re-ranking; current standard for retrieval-style NLP.

links.title: Argilla
links.description: open-source data annotation and feedback collection platform for LLM and NLP datasets.

links.title: HuggingFace Datasets
links.description: standardized loaders and processing for thousands of NLP datasets.

links.title: HuggingFace Evaluate
links.description: reference implementations for NLP metrics.

links.title: sacrebleu
links.description: reproducible BLEU/chrF/TER scoring for machine translation.

links.title: COMET
links.description: learned MT metrics, current de-facto standard.

links.title: LangTest
links.description: 60+ test types for NLP model robustness, bias, and fairness.

links.title: StarSpace
links.description: a library from Facebook for creating embeddings of word-level, paragraph-level, document-level and for text classification

links.title: RDRPOSTagger
links.description: A robust POS tagging toolkit available (in both Java & Python) together with pre-trained models for 40+ languages.

links.title: Kotidgy
links.description: an index-based text data generator written in Kotlin

links.title: ATR4S
links.description: Toolkit with state-of-the-art [automatic term recognition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Terminology_extraction) methods.

links.title: tm
links.description: Implementation of topic modeling based on regularized multilingual [PLSA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Probabilistic_latent_semantic_analysis).

links.title: word2vec-scala
links.description: Scala interface to word2vec model; includes operations on vectors like word-distance and word-analogy.

links.title: Epic
links.description: Epic is a high performance statistical parser written in Scala, along with a framework for building complex structured prediction models.

links.title: text2vec
links.description: Fast vectorization, topic modeling, distances and GloVe word embeddings in R.

links.title: wordVectors
links.description: An R package for creating and exploring word2vec and other word embedding models

links.title: RMallet
links.description: R package to interface with the Java machine learning tool MALLET

links.title: dfr-browser
links.description: Creates d3 visualizations for browsing topic models of text in a web browser.

links.title: dfrtopics
links.description: R package for exploring topic models of text.

links.title: corporaexplorer
links.description: An R package for dynamic exploration of text collections

links.title: tidytext
links.description: Text mining using tidy tools

links.title: spacyr
links.description: R wrapper to spaCy NLP

links.title: CRAN Task View: Natural Language Processing
postagga
links.description: A library to parse natural language in Clojure and ClojureScript

links.title: prose
links.description: Text processing library supporting tokenization, part-of-speech tagging, and named-entity extraction.

links.title: nlp-engine
links.description: NLP++ engine to run NLP++ code on Linux including a full English parser

links.title: VisualText
links.description: Homepage for the NLP++ Language

links.title: NLP++ Wiki
links.description: Wiki entry for the NLP++ language

links.title: TextModels
links.description: Neural Network based models for Natural Language Processing

links.title: Word2Vec
links.description: Julia interface to word2vec

links.title: Wit-ai
links.description: Natural Language Interface for apps and devices

links.title: IBM Watson's Natural Language Understanding
links.description: API and Github demo

links.title: Amazon Comprehend
links.description: NLP and ML suite covers most common tasks like NER, tagging, and sentiment analysis

links.title: Google Cloud Natural Language API
links.description: Syntax Analysis, NER, Sentiment Analysis, and Content tagging in atleast 9 languages include English and Chinese (Simplified and Traditional).

links.title: ParallelDots
links.description: High level Text Analysis API Service ranging from Sentiment Analysis to Intent Analysis

links.title: Textalytic
links.description: Natural Language Processing in the Browser with sentiment analysis, named entity extraction, POS tagging, word frequencies, topic modeling, word clouds, and more

links.title: NLP Cloud
links.description: SpaCy NLP models (custom and pre-trained ones) served through a RESTful API for named entity recognition (NER), POS tagging, and more.

links.title: Cloudmersive
links.description: Unified and free NLP APIs that perform actions such as speech tagging, text rephrasing, language translation/detection, and sentence parsing

links.title: GATE
links.description: General Architecture and Text Engineering is 15+ years old, free and open source

links.title: brat
links.description: brat rapid annotation tool is an online environment for collaborative text annotation

links.title: doccano
links.description: doccano is free, open-source, and provides annotation features for text classification, sequence labeling and sequence to sequence

links.title: INCEpTION
links.description: A semantic annotation platform offering intelligent assistance and knowledge management

links.title: LightTag
links.description: Hosted and managed text annotation tool for teams, costs $

links.title: rstWeb
links.description: open source local or online tool for discourse tree annotations

links.title: GitDox
links.description: open source server annotation tool with GitHub version control and validation for XML data and collaborative spreadsheet grids

links.title: Konfuzio
links.description: team-first hosted and on-prem text, image and PDF annotation tool powered by active learning, freemium based, costs $

links.title: Shoonya
links.description: Shoonya is free and open source data annotation platform with wide varities of organization and workspace level management system. Shoonya is data agnostic, can be used by teams to annotate data with various level of verification stages at scale.

links.title: FLAT
links.description: FLAT is a web-based linguistic annotation environment based around the [FoLiA format](http://proycon.github.io/folia), a rich XML-based format for linguistic annotation. Free and open source.

links.title: Label Studio
links.description: open-core multi-modal labeling platform; widely used for NLP labeling.

links.title: Potato
links.description: Free, open-source annotation tool covering 21+ task types (classification, span, coreference, entity linking, agent trace evaluation) with built-in MACE quality control, attention checks, AI-assisted labeling, and 300+ example tasks.

links.title: word2vec
links.description: [implementation](https://code.google.com/archive/p/word2vec/) - [explainer blog](http://colah.github.io/posts/2014-07-NLP-RNNs-Representations/)

links.title: GloVe
links.description: [explainer blog](https://blog.acolyer.org/2016/04/22/glove-global-vectors-for-word-representation/)

links.title: fastText
links.description: [implementation](https://github.com/facebookresearch/fastText); subword n-grams handle OOV well, still useful for low-resource languages.

links.title: sense2vec
links.description: word sense disambiguation.

links.title: ELMo
links.description: deep contextualized word representations.

links.title: CoVe
links.description: contextualized vectors learned from MT.

links.title: ULMFiT
links.description: language-model fine-tuning for text classification.

links.title: InferSent
links.description: sentence representations from NLI.

links.title: UDPipe
links.description: tokenization, tagging, lemmatization, parsing for Universal Dependencies.

links.title: Morfessor
links.description: unsupervised morphological segmentation.

links.title: ByT5
links.description: tokenizer-free byte-level model.

links.title: CANINE
links.description: tokenization-free encoder operating on Unicode characters.

links.title: How Good is Your Tokenizer?
links.description: tokenizer fairness across languages.

links.title: The Token Tax: Systematic Bias in Multilingual Tokenization
Reducing Tokenization Premiums for Low-Resource Languages

links.description: foundational neural parsing architecture.

links.title: Trankit
links.description: light-weight transformer-based multilingual NLP toolkit.

links.title: Self-Attentive Constituency Parsing (Kitaev & Klein)
links.description: strong neural constituency parser.

links.description: canonical English NER benchmark.

links.description: jointly learns topic and document vectors.

links.description: robustly optimized BERT pretraining; common encoder baseline.

links.description: replaced-token-detection pretraining, sample-efficient.
